findingDetails.scopeProof: Recomputed the complete applicable scenario-row population from the linked report JSON rather than sampling cited examples.

findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The report may encode the intended population in a separate unexported gold or harness structure, but that structure is not evidence available in this report.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade when the report exposes the missing population/field or the analysis is revised to label the value as external and unverifiable.

focus: traceability — exact evidence-anchor and claim-citation audit

findingDetails.title: Literal null wording does not match omitted browser-row fields
findingDetails.evidence: All seven MR/CB browser objects exist and carry Mode-A scores, but has("liveEvidence") is false for each; DR-004 does contain liveEvidence.statedRoutingParsed=false at report line 2715. The semantic statement "no live evidence" is supported, while the literal serialized claim liveEvidence=null is not.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact-key inspection covered MR-001..MR-004 and CB-001..CB-003; every object returned hasLiveEvidence=false. Exact search separately found DR-004 statedRoutingParsed=false.
findingDetails.recommendation: Describe the seven rows as omitting liveEvidence (or as having no live evidence), unless the report serializer is changed to emit explicit null.
findingDetails.claim: R1-P1-001 warrants required-fix severity because the report cannot support claim (g).
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Inspected every MR/CB scenario object with exact has("liveEvidence") checks, projected the values, checked their Mode-A scores, and inspected DR-004 liveEvidence directly.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: jq projects a missing property as null, which explains how the draft arrived at liveEvidence=null even though the serialized JSON omits the key.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Resolve entirely if the draft replaces literal =null wording with omitted/no-live-evidence wording; restore P1 only if null-versus-omitted changes a downstream score or recommendation.

findingDetails.title: Twenty of twenty-two evidence anchors do not fully support their citing sentence
findingDetails.evidence: All 22 markdown report links resolve, but 12 cited lines contain only scenarioId and 8 contain only aggregateScore. Those 20 anchors do not expose the field/value sets claimed by their sentences. Only the two Tier-1 score links on line 91 fully support their linked numeric values.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Parsed every markdown link ending in .report.json:<line>, loaded the exact cited line plus adjacent lines, and classified all 22 anchors as full, partial, or unsupported.
findingDetails.recommendation: Re-anchor each claim to the exact JSON field lines that establish every asserted value; use multiple anchors where a sentence joins aggregate, dimension, and population claims.
findingDetails.claim: The draft's evidence links provide line-specific support for the sentences and table cells that cite them.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Inspected the cited line and its immediate neighbors for all 22 links and allowed partial credit where aggregateScore supported one component.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The links may have been intended as block-entry pointers, but the required traceability contract treats the line suffix as a precise field/value anchor.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade when every load-bearing sentence has exact field/value anchors, or when the document explicitly adopts and documents block-range citation semantics.

findingDetails.title: Load-bearing quantitative claims and all four open questions lack report citations
findingDetails.evidence: Recommendation 6's 0/19, 18/19, 15/30, and 18/30 counts; recommendation 7's all-three-report unscored-mode-a claim; recommendation 10's holdout/fitted counts; multiple findings statistics; and all four Open Questions have no markdown report link. The underlying report gaps mostly exist, but readers cannot trace them from the draft.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Searched the complete 123-line draft for report links and mapped every quantitative or schema-gap sentence to an inline or paragraph-level anchor.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add exact report anchors to every quantitative/schema claim and each Open Question; cite a non-report review artifact for the claimed Claude verification and supplied-context contradiction.
findingDetails.claim: Every quantitative recommendation and report-gap question in the draft is traceable to cited report evidence.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked surrounding paragraphs for shared citations and verified the named holdout, unscored-dimension, and row-count fields directly in the reports.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The provenance paragraph and earlier section links may be intended as blanket sourcing, but they do not identify the evidence for later counts or external verification claims.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade when each listed sentence has a precise report anchor and external provenance statements cite their actual audit artifact.

results.summary: All 22 links resolve, but only 2 fully support their linked claim; 8 are partial aggregate anchors and 12 point only to scenario identifiers.
Eight provenance figures reproduce; claim (g) is semantically supported but uses literal null wording for omitted keys, and the verifier identity has no report-verifiable audit anchor.
Not applicable to an analytical findings document.
Not applicable to an analytical findings document.
No feature-catalog claim is in scope.
No playbook capability is in scope.

traceabilityChecks.summary: Core traceability is partial: report values generally reproduce, but exact anchors and per-claim citation coverage fail. Overlays are not applicable to this analytical file target.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: A non-trivial analytical target makes recommendations from five report artifacts and contains 22 line-addressed evidence links.

targetSelection.selectionReason: The draft names these five reports as its complete evidence base; every link and every named recommendation gap was audited.

searchLedger.invariant: A line-addressed citation must expose the field/value supporting its citing sentence.

searchActions.result: 22 links resolved: 2 full, 8 partial aggregate-only, 12 scenarioId-only.

searchLedger.rationale: Twenty anchors fail full sentence-level support.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Load-bearing counts and report-gap claims may be present without any report anchor.

searchActions.result: Named recommendation claims and all four Open Questions have no inline or paragraph-level report link.

searchLedger.rationale: Missing anchors prevent independent claim-to-report tracing.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Iteration 1 may have missed explicit liveEvidence fields on the seven browser rows.

searchActions.result: MR-001..MR-004 and CB-001..CB-003 all have hasLiveEvidence=false; Mode-A scores are 100/50. DR-004 has liveEvidence.statedRoutingParsed=false.

searchLedger.rationale: The semantic no-live-evidence claim is supported, but literal explicit-null wording is not; prior P1 is downgraded.

searchLedger.invariant: Verdicts, aggregate scores, dimension scores, and unscored markers must match report aggregates.

searchActions.result: Verified PASS/100, FAIL/20, CONDITIONAL/65; all listed D1intra/D2/D3/D5 values and unscored markers match.

searchLedger.rationale: Complete aggregate projections match the table.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No value mismatch found; only the table's citation precision is defective.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The harness recommendations may cite gaps that do not exist in the reports.

searchActions.result: Confirmed six mcp no-gold rows, sk-doc/sk-code gold-coverage gaps, D1inter/D4 unscored-mode-a, and null holdoutScore with fitted counts 19/30.

searchLedger.rationale: The gaps are real; their missing or imprecise citations are tracked separately.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Report projections confirm the recommendation premises.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The four Open Questions may invent gaps not present in the reports.

searchActions.result: Q1-Q3 and the report half of Q4 reflect real gaps; the supplied-context assertion is external to the five reports and needs its own provenance anchor.

searchLedger.rationale: The questions are substantively grounded, while their citation omissions are covered by R2-P1-002.

findingDetails.title: Operational recommendations escape the fitted-only evidence they later disclaim
findingDetails.claim: Recommendations 1-3 and the cross-skill synthesis read as operational/model conclusions, while the supporting sk-doc, sk-code, and both Tier-1 reports contain fitted scenarios only; recommendation 10 acknowledges the generalization limit but scopes the holdout remedy to sk-doc and sk-code.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked all five generalization blocks, the per-skill recommendation qualifier, the intervention wording in recommendation 3, and mcp-tooling's 4 fitted plus 2 holdout rows.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The language may be intended as a conservative safety policy rather than a performance estimate; the observed sk-doc failure rate can justify caution, but it still does not justify an unqualified model/configuration verdict.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Explicitly scope recommendations 1-3 and the cross-skill conclusion to this Luna xhigh/fast executor run, label them provisional pending holdouts/ablations, and extend recommendation 10's holdout rule to any Tier-1 evidence used for generalization.
findingDetails.recommendation: Separate observed results from policy: state the tested configuration once, qualify the operational rules as provisional, and include Tier-1 in the holdout requirement.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Compared the generalization object in every named report with all ten recommendations and the cross-skill synthesis.

findingDetails.title: Omitted D1/D2 proxy and D5 hard-gate semantics overstate independent evidence
findingDetails.claim: The analysis presents D1intra and D2 pairs as corroborating routing results without explaining that these reports give D2 the same router-replay-recall signal as D1intra, while D5 is a separate hard gate that passes for all Tier-2 reports.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Compared every report's dimensionScores, every scenario row's d1intra/d2 fields, intentRecall/liveResourceOnly populations, and gate object; checked whether any report broke the D1intra=D2 pattern.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: D1 and D2 are conceptually separate benchmark dimensions, but in these report instances D2 explicitly reuses the replay-recall proxy, so their equal scores are not independent confirmation.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Add a score-semantics section that identifies D2 as a proxy duplicate for these runs, explains intentRecall/liveResourceOnly behavior, and interprets D5 as contract/tool-surface gating rather than leaf-resource accuracy.
findingDetails.recommendation: Insert a short 'How to read these scores' block before the findings table and avoid citing D1/D2 pairs as two independent confirmations.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Complete-population extraction covered 73 Tier-2 rows and 18 Tier-1 runtime rows across all five reports.

findingDetails.title: The Open Questions section duplicates settled recommendation work
findingDetails.claim: Open Question 2 repeats recommendation 8, Open Question 3 repeats recommendation 9, and Open Question 4 mixes an external provenance discrepancy with the already-adjudicated liveEvidence wording issue.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Mapped each Open Question to recommendations 1-10 and to the active traceability findings; retained Question 1 as genuinely unresolved.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The section may be intended as a reader checklist, but it is labeled Open Questions and therefore implies undecided work.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Convert repeated questions into acceptance criteria under their recommendations and leave only genuinely unresolved questions in the section.
findingDetails.recommendation: Keep Question 1; move Questions 2-3 under recommendations 8-9 as validation criteria; split Question 4 into cited provenance follow-up and the existing wording correction.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exhaustive one-to-one mapping of all four questions against all ten recommendations.

spec_code.detail: Existing 2/22 exact-anchor coverage remains partial; this iteration adds recommendation-calibration and score-semantics evidence.

checklist_evidence.detail: Headline values remain reproduced, but provenance wording and recommendation evidence still require correction.

overlay.detail: File-target overlay protocols do not apply.

graphEvents.label: D1 D2 and D5 report fields

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: A 124-line analytical deliverable makes ten operational recommendations from five multi-scenario benchmark reports.

targetSelection.selectionReason: The draft names all five reports as its complete evidence base; every recommendation and omitted-signal check depends on this closed set.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Operational recommendation strength exceeds the report populations' generalization support.

searchActions.result: Recommendations 1-3 and the synthesis are unqualified; recommendation 10 acknowledges fitted-only evidence for only two Tier-2 skills.
sk-doc, sk-code, and both Tier-1 reports have holdoutCount=0; only the no-gold mcp control has holdouts.

searchLedger.rationale: The mismatch affects the operational scope of the final recommendations.

searchLedger.invariant: A downstream reader must be able to distinguish independent evidence from duplicated proxies and hard gates.

searchActions.queryOrPath: dimensionScores plus scenarioRows.dims across all five reports
searchActions.result: D1intra equals D2 in every report; D2 uses router-replay-recall, while D5 is a separate hard gate.
43 mcp/sk-doc/Tier-1 report rows have intentRecall=0 with liveResourceOnly=true; sk-code omits intentRecall on all 30 rows.

searchLedger.rationale: The omitted semantics can make duplicated dimensions look like corroboration.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The Open Questions section duplicates actions already expressed as recommendations.

searchActions.result: Question 2 maps to recommendation 8, Question 3 to recommendation 9, and Question 4 mixes provenance with an active wording correction.

searchLedger.rationale: Duplicated status makes downstream recommendation maintenance ambiguous.

searchLedger.hypothesis: An omitted scenario family or event/read anomaly reverses a recommendation.

searchActions.queryOrPath: all scenarioRows grouped by family, recall, eventCount, observedReads, parsed status
searchActions.result: The draft's read/event separations reproduce; RD/DR surface rows are already included, and omitted SA/TH/no-gold families do not reverse recommendations.

searchLedger.rationale: No omitted per-scenario anomaly changes the direction of recommendations 1-5.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Complete-population projections reproduced the stated family and activity patterns.

focus: correctness+traceability adversarial re-verification of all active P1 findings

findingDetails.title: Exact-line anchors are imprecise, but scenario block-start citations remain traceable
findingDetails.claim: Twenty of twenty-two links fail the evidence contract because the cited physical line does not itself serialize every field used by the citing sentence.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Recounted all 22 exact-line categories and adversarially sampled 10 anchors across aggregate links, Tier-1 scalar links, and scenario block starts; for scenario anchors, followed the identified JSON object to its supporting fields.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: A file:line citation to the scenarioId is an unambiguous structured-record start, a conventional and usable pointer even when supporting fields occur later in that object.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Satisfied: all sampled scenario-start anchors identify the correct supporting record, and the shared doctrine requires concrete file:line evidence without imposing exact-token-only semantics.
findingDetails.recommendation: Re-anchor combined aggregate claims to the relevant field lines or ranges for precision, but treat scenarioId block starts as valid block-level evidence.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Automated recount of all 22 links preserved the 2 full / 8 partial / 12 scenario-id-only taxonomy; a 10-anchor referee sample tested block-level support.

findingDetails.title: Load-bearing recommendation and provenance claims remain uncited
findingDetails.claim: The active list identifies every uncited load-bearing recommendation and question as recommendations 6, 7, and 10 plus all four Open Questions.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked each recommendation's Evidence paragraph, nearby paragraph-level links, all four Open Questions, and the provenance header for report or non-report source anchors.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Earlier report links may be intended as blanket sourcing for the whole document, and Open Questions may be read as prompts rather than assertions.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Add direct evidence anchors to recommendations 6, 7, 8, and 10 and each retained Open Question; cite an audit/context artifact for authorship and verification provenance or remove those externally unverifiable assertions.
findingDetails.recommendation: Expand the existing correction list to include recommendation 8 and the provenance header; recommendation 9 is cited but remains an anchor-precision issue rather than an absence issue.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Complete 124-line citation-presence scan across all ten recommendations, four Open Questions, and the provenance paragraph.

findingDetails.title: Cross-skill synthesis still exceeds fitted-only evidence, but recommendations 1-3 do not
findingDetails.claim: Recommendations 1-3 and the cross-skill synthesis all overreach because holdout coverage is absent.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Re-extracted all five generalization blocks and separately assessed recommendation 1's deterministic validation, recommendation 2's conservative unattended-use prohibition, recommendation 3's explicit intervention retest, and the tested-configuration scope in the provenance header.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Recommendations 1 and 2 are conservative operating policies under uncertainty, while recommendation 3 is explicitly a hypothesis to retest; none requires a holdout-backed performance estimate.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Scope the line 69-71 cross-skill conclusion to these fitted suites and the tested executor/runtime configuration, and extend recommendation 10's holdout requirement to Tier-1 evidence used for generalization. Recommendations 1-3 need no P1 remediation.
findingDetails.recommendation: Keep the conservative operational policies; qualify only the central cross-skill conclusion and the holdout plan.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All five generalization objects were compared: mcp-tooling is 4 fitted + 2 holdout, while sk-doc is 19 + 0, sk-code 30 + 0, and both Tier-1 reports 9 + 0.

findingDetails.title: D1/D2 proxy, D5 gate, and weighting semantics need clarification, not a required correction
findingDetails.claim: The draft presents D1intra and D2 as two independent confirmations and omits a material D5 failure signal.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Verified aggregate equality in all five reports, searched all row-level proxy labels, checked Tier-2 and Tier-1 D5/gate fields, inspected intentRecall/liveResourceOnly serialization, and reconstructed the non-gate point allocation from D1=13, D2=20, and D3=15.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The draft reports paired dimensions but never explicitly claims statistical independence; D5 passes in every report and does not reverse the leaf-resource findings that drive the recommendations.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Satisfied: the issue affects interpretive clarity, not the truth of the scenario-level evidence or the conservative recommendations.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add a score-semantics note: D1intra equals D2 in all five aggregates; live rows label D2 router-replay-recall; D1+D2 occupy 33/48 non-gate points; D5 is a separate hard gate (Tier-2 100, Tier-1 97) and never fails here.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Cross-report extraction covered all five dimension blocks and every serialized proxy/intent field; it also corrected the prior overstatement that sk-code omits intentRecall on all 30 rows.

spec_code.detail: Exact-line taxonomy remains 2 full, 8 partial, and 12 scenario-id-only. ScenarioId links are accepted as usable structured-block anchors, reducing the defect to P2 precision.

checklist_evidence.detail: Recommendations 6, 7, 8, and 10 plus all four Open Questions lack direct citations; authorship/verification provenance needs a non-report source.

generalization.detail: Only mcp-tooling has holdouts (4 fitted + 2 holdout), but no routing gold; the other four reports have holdoutScore=null and holdoutCount=0.

measurement_semantics.detail: D1intra equals D2 in all five reports; D5 is 100 for Tier-2 and 97 for Tier-1 with gateFailed=false; point allocation and intent/proxy serialization were rechecked.

omission_sweep.detail: Tier-1 D5=97, intentRecall null-vs-absent semantics, and aggregate point weighting fold into R3-P1-002; no additional P1 was found.

overlay.detail: Skill/agent/runtime overlay protocols do not apply to this analytical file target.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Adversarial adjudication of four active P1 findings across a 124-line analytical deliverable and five multi-scenario reports.

targetSelection.selectionReason: The prompt fixed the closed evidence set and required re-verification of every active P1 against raw report JSON.

searchLedger.invariant: A citation must identify a concrete supporting report location; structured-record starts may supply block-level support.

searchActions.result: 22 links: 2 full, 8 partial, 12 scenario-id-only.

searchActions.queryOrPath: 10 anchors spanning aggregate, scalar, and scenario-start links
searchActions.result: Every sampled scenarioId anchor identified the correct supporting JSON object.

searchLedger.rationale: The exact-line precision defect remains, but block-start semantics reduce it to P2.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The active uncited-claim list omits at least one load-bearing recommendation or provenance statement.

searchActions.result: Recommendations 6, 7, 8, and 10 and all four Open Questions lack direct links; provenance has no audit/context citation.

searchLedger.rationale: The list is materially incomplete and the analytical deliverable still lacks traceability for actionable claims.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Fitted-only populations invalidate both the operational policies and the cross-skill conclusion.

searchActions.queryOrPath: generalization objects in all five reports
searchActions.result: mcp-tooling 4+2; sk-doc 19+0; sk-code 30+0; Tier-1 opencode 9+0; Tier-1 codex 9+0.
Recommendations 1-2 are conservative policies and recommendation 3 explicitly requests an intervention retest.

searchLedger.rationale: The P1 survives only for the central cross-skill synthesis and incomplete holdout plan.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Duplicated proxy and omitted hard-gate semantics create a required correctness defect.

searchActions.queryOrPath: dimensionScores and gate objects in all five reports
searchActions.result: D1intra equals D2 throughout; D5 is 100 in Tier-2 and 97 in Tier-1, hardGate=true, gateFailed=false.

searchActions.queryOrPath: proxy, intentRecall, liveResourceOnly, and dimension point fields
searchActions.result: The semantic omission is real, but the target makes no explicit independence claim and its scenario-level recommendations remain supported.

searchLedger.rationale: Interpretive clarity warrants P2; no recommendation-reversing error was found.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Tier-1 D5, intentRecall serialization, or aggregate weighting creates an additional active P1.

searchActions.queryOrPath: all five report dimension and row-metric blocks
searchActions.result: Signals expand the measurement-semantics advisory; none contradicts the scenario-level findings or adds a separate P1.

searchLedger.rationale: All remaining signals are consolidated into R3-P1-002.

measurement_semantics.detail: Adjusted wording distinguishes the shared D1intra/D2 proxy, D5 hard-gate conformance, and intentRecall serialization.

recommendation_synthesis.detail: All ten draft recommendations received dispositions; two additions cover score semantics and evidence-anchor/provenance rules.

traceabilityChecks.summary: Core traceability remains partial until the read-only target adopts the adjusted wording and citations; overlays are not applicable.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: A multi-report analytical target with operational recommendations, provenance claims, and cross-suite generalization is non-trivial.

targetSelection.selectionReason: These are the declared review target and the five primary reports from which every adjusted recommendation must derive.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Cross-skill and operational wording can escape fitted-only evidence.

searchActions.result: The draft uses model-wide synthesis and Tier-1 confirms language.

searchActions.queryOrPath: generalization blocks in all five reports
searchActions.result: Only mcp-tooling has holdouts; the four evidentiary routing suites report holdoutCount 0.

searchLedger.rationale: Adjusted wording is supplied in this narrative, but the reviewed target is read-only.
searchLedger.deferredReason: Apply the scoped wording in a separate implementation/edit pass.

searchLedger.invariant: Every recommendation and retained open question must have claim-complete evidence anchors.

searchActions.result: Recommendations 6, 7, 8, and 10 and all four questions require direct citations; existing anchors mix aggregate, scenario-block, and scenario-id semantics.

searchLedger.rationale: The narrative supplies citations and a precise anchor convention without mutating the target.
searchLedger.deferredReason: Transfer the adjusted list and cited questions into the target during follow-up.

searchLedger.invariant: Readers must not treat D1intra and D2 as independent confirmations or D5 as leaf-routing accuracy.

searchActions.result: D1intra equals D2 in all reports, D5 passes at 100 or 97, and intentRecall is zero or absent under liveResourceOnly semantics.

searchLedger.rationale: A ready-to-insert reading guide is supplied below.
searchLedger.deferredReason: The guide must be inserted into the read-only analysis in a separate edit pass.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Report JSONs cannot prove analysis authorship or verifier identity.

searchActions.queryOrPath: report metadata and target provenance statement
searchActions.result: Reports establish benchmark subject and results, not who authored or verified the analysis.

searchLedger.rationale: Narrow replacement wording is supplied without asserting missing provenance.
searchLedger.deferredReason: Attach a non-report audit citation or adopt the narrower provenance text.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Final synthesis may introduce a new P0/P1 beyond the adjudicated register.

searchActions.queryOrPath: adjudicated active register plus recommendation-by-recommendation synthesis
searchActions.result: No new blocker or required finding emerged; changes map to the two active P1s and four active P2s.

searchLedger.rationale: The synthesis changes wording and evidence coverage without discovering a new defect class.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: All material issues are already represented by the adjudicated active register.

reason: stop_policy=max-iterations ceiling reached at iteration 5 of 5
